Title: How do conifers biosynthesize their complex oleoresin terpene defenses?

Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Conifer terpene synthases (TPS) are the principal source of oleoresin terpene structural diversity. 2. Many conifer TPS are multiproduct and stereospecific enzymes, generating large terpene diversity from common prenyl diphosphate substrates. 3. TPS genes show resin-duct/cell-type-specific and methyl jasmonate-inducible expression associated with constitutive and induced oleoresin defense.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Preferentially expressed in cortical resin duct (CRD) cells; induced in stems or needles by methyl jasmonate, wounding, insect feeding or oviposition; some localized in epithelial cells of traumatic resin ducts (TRDs).
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Plastid or cytosol depending on TPS type; some predicted mitochondrial targeting; experimentally confirmed plastid localization reported for Pinus taeda abietadiene synthase.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes biosynthesis and diversification of conifer oleoresin mono-, sesqui-, di-, and hemiterpenes involved in defense and ecological interactions.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: TPS genes are candidates for engineering terpene composition for pest resistance and for bioproduct production, but many are multiproduct enzymes so deployment requires careful functional validation.
Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: PG29; other spruce genotypes including resistant and susceptible Sitka spruce genotypes
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Methyl jasmonate treatment; mechanical wounding; insect feeding; oviposition; pathogen attack
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Multiproduct activities complicate prediction and engineering of specific product outcomes; defense effectiveness can become insufficient under climate-driven pest outbreaks.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This is a review article; TPS is treated here as a summarized core enzyme family rather than a single experimentally tested gene in one study.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense against herbivores and pathogens; volatile ecological signaling; pheromone precursor supply for co-evolved insects; possible nutrient source for insect-associated microbiomes.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome annotation; transcriptome; proteome; RNA-seq including laser microdissection-based cell-type transcriptomics
Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Picea spp.; Pinus spp.; Abies spp.; Thuja plicata; Callitropsis nootkatensis; Taxus spp.
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Terpene synthase family; gymnosperm-specific TPS-d subfamily for specialized metabolism; class-I, class-II, or bifunctional class-I/II domains
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant enzyme assays; metabolite profiling; transcript analysis; proteomics; localization studies; structure-function analysis; mutagenesis
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Oleoresin in cortical resin ducts and traumatic resin ducts; volatile emissions from needles

Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP720B cytochromes P450 expand diterpene diversity beyond TPS-generated scaffolds. 2. Clade III CYP720B enzymes are multisubstrate multifunctional oxidases converting diterpene olefins to diterpene alcohols, aldehydes and resin acids. 3. Clade I CYP720B enzymes oxidize 13-hydroxy-8(14)-abietene from LAS-type di-TPS, and together CYP720B enzymes form a matrix-like system with di-TPS to generate diverse DRAs.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Preferentially expressed in resin duct cells, especially cortical resin ducts; PsCYP720B4 is preferentially expressed in CRD and induced by methyl jasmonate.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes oxidation and diversification of diterpene scaffolds into defense-related diterpene alcohols, aldehydes and resin acids.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering DRA composition and resistance-associated diterpenes such as dehydroabietic acid; best combined with matching di-TPS because product output depends on enzyme combinations.
Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: PG29; Sitka spruce and other spruce/pine genotypes
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Multisubstrate oxidation may yield broad metabolite shifts rather than single-product outcomes.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review-level summary across clades I-IV; only a subset of CYP720B genes have been functionally characterized.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Chemical defense against insects and pathogens; contributes to viscous resin that traps insects.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome annotation; transcriptome; laser microdissection RNA-seq
Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Picea abies; Picea sitchensis; Picea glauca; Pinus spp.
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cytochrome P450 monooxygenase, conifer-specific CYP720B subfamily
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression and enzyme assays; transcript profiling; metabolite profiling

Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Methyl jasmonate inducible in Pinus densiflora and in Norway spruce type-II DXS genes/cell cultures
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Ginkgo biloba; Pinus densiflora; Picea abies
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Precursor supply for plastidial terpene biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses pyruvate and glyceraldehyde-3-phosphate to form 1-deoxy-D-xylulose-5-phosphate.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DXS is a functionally characterized conifer/gymnosperm MEP-pathway enzyme. 2. Multiple DXS genes occur in Norway spruce, with type-II DXS genes being methyl jasmonate inducible. 3. DXS contributes precursor supply for induced monoterpene and diterpene oleoresin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Latin_Name: Ginkgo biloba; Pinus densiflora; Picea abies
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome; cell culture transcript analysis; laser microdissection transcriptomics
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: MeJA inducible; multiple DXS genes in Norway spruce
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: MEP pathway transcripts preferentially expressed in cortical resin duct cells of white spruce bark
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports flux into oleoresin monoterpene and diterpene biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to increase precursor supply for terpene defenses, but pathway-level engineering may be required.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Three different DXS genes known in Norway spruce; type-II genes specifically emphasized as inducible.

Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Methyl jasmonate inducible in Norway spruce cell cultures
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Precursor supply for plastidial terpene biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts 1-deoxy-D-xylulose-5-phosphate to methylerythritol phosphate.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Norway spruce DXR is MeJA inducible. 2. As part of the MEP pathway, DXR supports precursor formation for oleoresin mono- and diterpenes.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: MeJA inducible in Norway spruce cell cultures
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: MEP pathway transcripts preferential in CRD cells in white spruce bark
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports induced terpene precursor biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential precursor-supply target.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review summary only.

Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Functional characterization in ginkgo; pathway transcripts enriched in CRD cells in white spruce
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Ginkgo biloba; Picea abies; Picea glauca
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Precursor supply for plastidial terpene biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts ME-cPP to HMBPP in the MEP pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HDS has been characterized in ginkgo. 2. MEP-pathway expression pattern supports a role in conifer oleoresin precursor supply.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Preferentially expressed in CRD cells as part of MEP pathway transcriptome
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports precursor formation for monoterpene/diterpene defense metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential upstream flux target.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review summary only.

Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Methyl jasmonate inducible in Pinus densiflora and Norway spruce cell cultures
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Ginkgo biloba; Pinus densiflora; Picea abies
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Precursor supply for plastidial terpene biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces HMBPP to IPP and DMAPP.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HDR is a functionally characterized gymnosperm/conifer MEP enzyme. 2. HDR transcripts are MeJA inducible in pine and spruce systems. 3. HDR supports precursor production for induced oleoresin terpene biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Latin_Name: Ginkgo biloba; Pinus densiflora; Picea abies
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: MEP pathway preferential in CRD cells
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increases availability of IPP/DMAPP for terpene biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for upstream precursor enhancement.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Produces both IPP and DMAPP in pathway context.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Cytosolic precursor supply for sesquiterpene biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses acetyl-CoA-derived substrates to form HMG-CoA in the mevalonate pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Two different ginkgo HMGS genes with different tissue expression were functionally validated by complementation of HMGS-deficient yeast. 2. MEV-pathway genes generally support sesquiterpene precursor supply.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Two HMGS genes showed different tissue expression in ginkgo
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: MEV pathway transcripts showed similar expression across stem cortex cell types in white spruce
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports mevalonate-pathway precursor formation for sesquiterpenes.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Possible target for sesquiterpene precursor engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Expression not preferential in CRD compared with MEP genes.

Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Epithelial cells of traumatic resin ducts
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Bifunctional homodimeric IDS producing both GPP and GGPP from IPP/DMAPP.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PaIDS1 is a bifunctional GGPPS/GPPS enzyme. 2. It supplies both monoterpene and diterpene precursors. 3. PaIDS1 transcripts strongly increase in bark and xylem after MeJA treatment and protein localizes to epithelial cells of traumatic resin ducts.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Dual activity may complicate selective engineering toward one terpene class.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Strongly induced in bark and xylem after MeJA treatment
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Localized in epithelial cells of TRDs
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key precursor-supply step but not explicitly called rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances precursor supply for induced oleoresin monoterpene and diterpene biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Attractive target for boosting broad terpene defenses in induced tissues.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Homodimeric and bifunctional.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Produces geranyl diphosphate precursor for monoterpene biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PaIDS2 has GPPS activity. 2. It is MeJA inducible. 3. Expression in bark and xylem suggests a role in oleoresin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in bark and xylem; induced by MeJA
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Bark and xylem expression consistent with oleoresin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports monoterpene precursor formation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate to enhance monoterpene defenses.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One of several PaIDS with characterized function.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Produces farnesyl diphosphate precursor for sesquiterpene biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PaIDS4 has FPPS activity. 2. It is among MeJA-inducible PaIDS genes supporting induced terpene metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: NA
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports sesquiterpene precursor formation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for altering sesquiterpene blends.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review summary only.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Produces geranylgeranyl diphosphate precursor for diterpene biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PaIDS5 has GGPPS activity. 2. It is expressed in bark and xylem in patterns suggesting a role in oleoresin biosynthesis. 3. PaIDS genes are induced by MeJA.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in bark and xylem; MeJA inducible
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expression pattern consistent with oleoresin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports diterpene precursor formation for oleoresin defenses.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful upstream target for DRA production engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: NA

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Produces geranylgeranyl diphosphate precursor for diterpene biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PaIDS6 has GGPPS activity. 2. PaIDS genes are induced by MeJA and contribute to precursor supply for oleoresin terpene biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: NA
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports diterpene precursor formation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential upstream engineering target.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: NA

Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Related to spruce weevil resistance; genotype-dependent copy number/presence-absence variation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes GPP to produce (+)-3-carene.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PsTPS-3car2 presence and activity distinguish resistant versus susceptible Sitka spruce genotypes. 2. Its expression and higher catalytic efficiency account for relatively high (+)-3-carene levels in the resistant genotype. 3. (+)-3-Carene is associated with resistance to spruce weevil.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Copy-number and allelic variation can create strong genotype dependence of trait expression.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Present/expressed in weevil-resistant genotype; absent in susceptible genotype
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected differences in genomes, transcriptomes and proteomes of resistant vs susceptible genotypes
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Major determinant of (+)-3-carene abundance in this genotype comparison
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes (+)-3-carene accumulation associated with weevil resistance.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value marker/engineering target for increasing resistance-associated (+)-3-carene in Sitka spruce.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: weevil-resistant and weevil-susceptible Sitka spruce genotypes
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Compared with PsTPS-3car1/-3 and PsTPS-sab; minor amino-acid changes alter product specificity and efficiency.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene copy-number analysis; transcriptomics; proteomics; recombinant enzyme assays; metabolite profiling; structure-function analysis

Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Genotype-dependent and structure/function variation related to weevil-resistance study
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes GPP to produce (-)-sabinene.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PsTPS-sab is closely related to PsTPS-3car genes. 2. Minor amino acid variation helps determine (+)-3-carene versus (-)-sabinene formation and catalytic efficiency.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Small sequence changes can strongly shift product outcome, complicating prediction.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in comparative genotype analysis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to monoterpene variation through alternative product formation relative to 3-carene synthases.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful model for rational engineering of monoterpene product specificity.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper frames this together with PsTPS-3car variants as an example of TPS plasticity.

Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Induced terpenoid defense; substrate for CYP720B oxidation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Bifunctional class-I/II di-TPS cyclizing GGPP via (+)-CPP to form primarily 13-hydroxy-8(14)-abietene, which dehydrates to abietadiene/levopimaradiene/neoabietadiene/palustradiene.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PaLAS is a bifunctional di-TPS central to abietane-type diterpene scaffold formation. 2. Its actual enzymatic product is 13-hydroxy-8(14)-abietene rather than only olefins. 3. This product and its dehydration products are substrates for CYP720B enzymes to generate multiple DRAs.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: LAS-type genes preferentially expressed in resin duct cells in induced systems
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key scaffold-forming step for major DRA branch
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes production of abietane-type diterpene precursors for major resin acids.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important target for engineering DRA composition; should be combined with specific CYP720B enzymes.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Four amino acids differentiate LAS and Iso product profiles.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Detailed mechanistic enzyme study; structure-function analysis

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Bifunctional class-I/II di-TPS converting GGPP to abietane-type diterpene scaffold intermediates.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AgLAS was the first cloned and characterized conifer di-TPS. 2. It established foundational knowledge for structure and mechanism of conifer and plant di-TPS.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: NA
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Generates diterpene scaffolds for DRA biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Structural template for diterpene engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Protein structure used as canonical model in the review.

Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Methyl jasmonate inducible; preferentially expressed in cortical resin ducts
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes sequential oxidation of diterpene scaffolds to diterpene alcohols, aldehydes and resin acids; in Sitka spruce also produces dehydroabietic acid.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP720B4 is a multifunctional diterpene-oxidizing P450 in Sitka spruce. 2. It is preferentially expressed in CRD and induced in defense contexts. 3. It produces dehydroabietic acid, a DRA associated with weevil resistance.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Broad substrate range may alter multiple DRA outputs simultaneously.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Spatially refined transcript analysis using laser microdissection
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Preferentially expressed in CRD; induced under defense conditions
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: CRD-preferential in MeJA-induced Sitka spruce stems
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key diversification step in DRA branch
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increases DRA diversity and dehydroabietic acid accumulation associated with resistance.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for engineering resistance-associated DRA blends, especially with matching di-TPS partners.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functions within a modular matrix-like combination system with di-TPS.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Functional characterization; spatial transcript analysis; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Diterpene alcohols; diterpene aldehydes; diterpene resin acids including dehydroabietic acid

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Sequentially oxidizes multiple diterpene olefins to corresponding alcohols, aldehydes and resin acids.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP720B1 is a clade III multifunctional, multisubstrate diterpene oxidase. 2. It participates in matrix-like production of multiple resin acids from diterpene olefin scaffolds.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: NA
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Expands DRA chemical diversity.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for DRA pathway engineering with appropriate scaffold-producing di-TPS.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review does not specify one single applied species context here.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Abietic acid; neoabietic acid; levopimaric acid; palustric acid
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes 13-hydroxy-8(14)-abietene produced by LAS-type di-TPS into multiple diterpene resin acids.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP720B2 is a clade I enzyme inactive with diterpene olefins. 2. Instead, it converts 13-hydroxy-8(14)-abietene from LAS-type di-TPS into multiple DRAs.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Substrate specificity limited to LAS-derived alcohol scaffold.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: NA
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Channels LAS products into major abietane resin acids.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful when paired specifically with LAS-type enzymes for targeted DRA production.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Inactive with diterpene olefins unlike clade III enzymes.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Abietic acid; neoabietic acid; levopimaric acid; palustric acid
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes 13-hydroxy-8(14)-abietene from LAS-type di-TPS to multiple diterpene resin acids.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP720B12 is a clade I enzyme that acts on the LAS-derived alcohol intermediate rather than diterpene olefins. 2. It contributes to matrix-like production of major abietane DRAs.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Depends on LAS-type upstream scaffold provision.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: NA
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to production of major abietane DRAs.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Pair with LAS-type scaffolds for DRA engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Similar functional logic to CYP720B2.

Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Foliage resin gland-specific context; identified through genotype comparison with or without foliar resin glands
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates (+)-sabinene at C3 as the second step after sabinene synthase in α-thujone biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP750B1 is a conifer-specific P450 catalyzing the second step of α-thujone biosynthesis. 2. It was discovered via differential expression analysis in genotypes with versus without foliar resin glands. 3. It contributes to thujone accumulation in foliage glands linked to herbivore deterrence.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Reference transcriptome; differential gene expression analysis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Associated with foliage resin gland-containing genotypes
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in foliage resin gland context
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key pathway step after sabinene synthase
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes α-thujone biosynthesis in foliage resin glands.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to enhance browsing deterrence in foliage.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Genotypes with or without foliar resin glands
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts after (+)-sabinene synthase in western redcedar.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes two-step oxidation of (+)-valencene to (+)-nootkatone.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP706M1 converts (+)-valencene to (+)-nootkatone through two-step oxidation. 2. The product is proposed to confer resistance to heartwood rot fungi and has fragrance value.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heartwood-specific system
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes nootkatone accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Valuable for fragrance and potential disease-resistance engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Product has both ecological and industrial value.

Pathway_Genes.Gene_Name: CYP725A1; CYP725A2; CYP725A3; CYP725A4; CYP725A5; CYP725A6
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylate the taxadiene scaffold at various positions during taxol biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Multiple gymnosperm-specific CYP725A enzymes hydroxylate taxadiene at different positions. 2. These oxidations are central to taxol biosynthesis in yew.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: NA
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enable elaboration of taxane scaffold toward taxol.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important pathway modules for pharmaceutical biosynthesis engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review mentions these as established examples beyond oleoresin defense.
